sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Cercle Brugge is struggling, with no wins in their last 6 matches and no goals scored in their last 3 games. KVC Westerlo has had mixed results, with 2 wins and 3 losses in their last 5 matches, showing inconsistency but some scoring ability.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, results were split with a 1-1 draw and a 3-0 win for Westerlo. Westerlo seems to have a slight edge, especially with Cercle Brugge's inability to keep clean sheets against them.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Cercle Brugge has failed to score this season (0 goals) and ranks poorly in attack, though defensively they are decent (4 goals conceded). Westerlo has scored 5 goals but conceded 7, indicating vulnerability at the back but better attacking output.

sections.subtitle: Key Trends
sections.text: Cercle Brugge's matches often see under 2.5 goals, while Westerlo's games frequently have over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring. This suggests Westerlo might push for goals, while Cercle may struggle offensively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Cercle Brugge leads KVC Westerlo with a commanding 3-0 scoreline at Jan Breydel Stadium in Bruges. The home side has been clinical in front of goal, converting their chances with three well-taken strikes in the first half. KVC Westerlo, despite having more possession and more entries into the final third, has failed to capitalize on their opportunities, leaving them with a mountain to climb in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Cercle Brugge has shown great efficiency in attack, scoring three goals from a handful of big chances created. Their forwards have been sharp, with notable contributions from key players finding the back of the net. Defensively, they’ve held firm, limiting Westerlo’s ability to convert their possession into clear scoring opportunities. The home side also demonstrated composure in duels, winning a significant portion of their ground battles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: KVC Westerlo has struggled to make their dominance in possession count, failing to create any big chances despite numerous entries into the penalty area. Their defense has been porous, conceding three goals in quick succession towards the end of the half. While they’ve shown promise in aerial duels and have had several corner kicks, their inability to test the Cercle Brugge goalkeeper consistently has been a major concern.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw a clear shift in attacking momentum between the two sides. Early on, KVC Westerlo dominated the attacking spells, consistently pushing forward and maintaining pressure in the opposition half. However, Cercle Brugge gradually gained control, especially in the latter stages of the half, with sustained periods of attacking intent that ultimately led to their three goals. Westerlo had a late flurry of pressure, but it wasn’t enough to break through the home defense before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: For Cercle Brugge, players like Alan Minda and Steve Ngoura have been pivotal, not only scoring but also contributing to the team’s attacking dynamism. Their ability to exploit spaces in the Westerlo defense has been crucial. On the other hand, KVC Westerlo will need more from their creative players in midfield to unlock Cercle Brugge’s backline. Tactically, Westerlo might push for an early goal in the second half to regain some confidence, while Cercle Brugge could look to manage the game and protect their lead.
